2604.08418.title: Exploring Temporal Representation in Neural Processes for Multimodal Action Prediction
2604.08418.paper_summary: Inspired by the human ability to understand and predict others, we study the applicability of Conditional Neural Processes (CNP) to the task of self-supervised multimodal action prediction in robotics. Following recent results regarding the ontogeny of the Mirror Neuron System (MNS), we focus on the preliminary objective of self-actions prediction. We find a good MNS-inspired model in the existing Deep Modality Blending Network (DMBN), able to reconstruct the visuo-motor sensory signal during a partially observed action sequence by leveraging the probabilistic generation of CNP. After a qualitative and quantitative evaluation, we highlight its difficulties in generalizing to unseen action sequences, and identify the cause in its inner representation of time. Therefore, we propose a revised version, termed DMBN-Positional Time Encoding (DMBN-PTE), that facilitates learning a more robust representation of temporal information, and provide preliminary results of its effectiveness in expanding the applicability of the architecture. DMBN-PTE figures as a first step in the development of robotic systems that autonomously learn to forecast actions on longer time scales refining their predictions with incoming observations.
2604.08418.paper_summary_zh: 受到人類理解和預測他人的能力啟發，我們研究條件神經過程（CNP）在機器人自我監督多模態行動預測任務中的適用性。根據最近關於鏡像神經系統（MNS）發育的研究結果，我們專注於自我行動預測的初步目標。我們在現有的深度模態融合網絡（DMBN）中找到了一個良好的受MNS啟發的模型，該模型能夠通過利用CNP的概率生成，重建在部分觀察的行動序列中的視覺-運動感覺信號。在進行質性和量化評估後，我們強調了其在對未見行動序列進行泛化方面的困難，並確定了其內部時間表示的原因。因此，我們提出了一個修訂版本，稱為DMBN-位置時間編碼（DMBN-PTE），該版本促進了對時間信息的更穩健表示的學習，並提供了其在擴展架構適用性方面的初步效果結果。DMBN-PTE作為開發能夠自主學習在更長時間尺度上預測行動的機器人系統的第一步，隨著新觀察的到來不斷完善其預測。
2604.08418.author: Marco Gabriele Fedozzi et.al.
2604.08418.authors: Marco Gabriele Fedozzi, Yukie Nagai, Francesco Rea, Alessandra Sciutti
